sourceMovie.title: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming for the same reason it broke through in 2019: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tim Johnson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming clicked for you, Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Wish Dragon

faq.question: What should I watch after How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming?
faq.answer: Try Raya and the Last Dragon, Wish Dragon, Dragons: Dawn of the Dragon Racers—each shares DNA with How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming?
faq.answer: How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming (2019) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Adventure, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is How to Train Your Dragon: Homecoming worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
